content.full_text:
NATIONAL ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT & LAW CENTER
1950 ADDISON STREET • BERKELEY, CALIFORNIA 94704
415•548•2600


June 29, 1990

Luisa Menchaca
Counsel, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J. Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Menchaca:

I am writing at the suggestion of Ann Harrington regarding the
application of proposed regulation 18703.1 to the South Berkeley
Community Housing Development Corporation (SBCHDC).

The relevant facts are as follows:

     a. SBCHDC operates within a portion of the South Berkeley
Area Planning District. The city of Berkeley tells me
that this is composed of census tracts 4234, 4235, 4239
and 4240. Carole Norris of the city informs me that the
population of these census tracts is less than 25,000 and
that the total area is less then ten square miles.

     b. The bylaws of the corporation do not provide any
requirement that the board members reside within the
planning district or any other particular geographic
area.

     c. None of the board members are elected public
officials.

     d. No one owns property that is directly affected by
activities of the corporation.

     e. Six board members do have a principal residence within
the jurisdiction of the SBCHDC, but none have their
residence, I am told, within 300 feet of the boundaries
of the property which is the subject of the corporation's
proposed development.

     f. The principal residences, I am told, are located on
parcel's of land not more than one-quarter acre in size.

Luisa Menchaca                    -2-                    June 29, 1990

     g. In addition to the two board members previously
referred to, the corporation has a third board member who
owns commercial property and operates a small business
directly opposite the property to be developed by the
corporation.

Based on the above information, we would request an opinion as to
whether or not the proposed regulation will have any impact on
SBCHDC and would appreciate consideration of any steps that could
be taken to make the operation of the corporation more manageable
in the event that their actions are not exempted by the proposed
regulation. If further information is necessary for you to make
a determination, please let me know, and I will do my best to get
you what you need. I appreciate your assistance in finding a way
to resolve this difficulty for the client group.

Very truly yours

[signature]

DAVID H. KIRKPATRICK
Attorney at Law

cc: Ann Harrington

sbchdclm.wp

90-445

FPPC
JUL 6 1990

NATIONAL ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT & LAW CENTER
1950 ADDISON STREET • BERKELEY, CALIFORNIA 94704
415•548•2600


July 2, 1990

Luisa Menchaca
Counsel, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J. Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Menchaca:

Enclosed is a replacement for a letter I sent you last Friday. You
will note that there is a slight change in paragraph g. I hope
that this will make the request for an opinion clearer.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

DAVID H. KIRKPATRICK
Attorney at Law

cc: Ann Harrington

The relevant facts are as follows: a. SBCHDC operates within a portion of the South Berkeley Area Planning District. The city of Berkeley tells me that this is composed of census tracts 4234, 4235, 4239 and 4240. Carole Norris of the city informs me that the population of these census tracts is less than 25,000 and that the total area is less then ten square miles. b. The bylaws of the corporation do not provide any requirement that the board members reside within the planning district or any other particular geographic area. c. None of the board members are elected public officials. d. No one owns property that is directly affected by activities of the corporation. e. Six board members do have a principal residence within the jurisdiction of the SBCHDC, but none have their residence, I am told, within 300 feet of the boundaries of the property which is the subject of the corporation's proposed development. f.
